title: Critical Connections in a Network (Bridges)
coreIdea: Find all edges whose removal would disconnect the graph (bridges), by running DFS while tracking discovery time and the lowest reachable ancestor time for each node; an edge is a bridge if the child's lowest reachable time is strictly greater than the parent's discovery time.
recognitionCues: asks for critical edges/connections whose removal disconnects the graph
undirected connected graph, single-pass DFS solution expected
approach: DFS from any node, tracking discovery time and low-link value for each node. For each neighbor: if unvisited, recurse then update current node's low-link with child's low-link, and if child's low-link > current node's discovery time, the edge is a bridge. If neighbor is visited and not the direct parent, update low-link with neighbor's discovery time.
